rationale: The cited paper is directly in the prior stream the citing paper claims to extend: it focuses on timing of durable-goods replacement (including forced vs. unforced decisions), while the citing paper explicitly says prior upgrade/replacement work focused on replacement choice timing and that it shifts to behavior toward current possessions. That makes this citation plausibly a foundational/comparison source rather than an incidental mention, despite limited raw reference context (DOI-only match).
